payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, to 826 and deliver all your wonderful donations. Last announcement I have to make. Let's see. The wonderful folks at Midnight Syndicate-- for those who don't know who they are, they make fantastic music and soundtrack tracks. They've done an official DND soundtrack years ago, and they've done stuff for World of Darkness and Call of Cthulhu. They do great atmospheric music. They finally got back to me, and they gave me permission to use their songs on this feed, so my playlist expands even further. They're really cool guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Only because of the dice, not because of your choices.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Too late!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, guys. Just like we did in rehearsals. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So 826LA, our charity. Everyone should go check them out; they have amazing storefronts in cities all across the country. The one here is themed after time travel in Echo Park. And also in Marina Del Rey; that's the other city that I'm trying to think of. But yes, a portion of all of our donations go to them, and they're amazing, so thank you so much. And thank you for everyone who has donated in the past. We love you. And I think we are going to be doing a video. I know I've been saying that for a while, but we have a date scheduled now! It is happening. We have a way for everyone to get back in town, but it's going to happen, and we're going to have a field trip.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Eight and five and four. 17 damage. (impact, growling) You can see now it has the two giant tentacles with these jagged spines that look covered in whatever it's been climbing in and this one central stalk that has eyes across it, and the eyes flicker in pain and wince and then focus in on you and looks across your companions. Do you wish to move, or do you want to stay where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16. Okay, it does not make it. As you bring out your holy symbol for a moment from beneath your armor, you look forward and thrust your spear forward. This energy travels from the middle of your torso, across your arm, down the spear, and fires off as this burst of vibrant whitish-blue light that blasts into the front of this otyugh creature. You deal 3d8 damage to it. So go ahead and roll eight-sided dice three times.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL

preceding_turns.text: Big money, no whammies.

preceding_turns.text: That's really good.

preceding_turns.text: Wonderful, so let's see. Here's a six.

preceding_turns.text: Hold on, I'm going to spend one sorcery point to empower the spell, and I'm going to pick the six and reroll these four dice, because I can reroll up to that many when I empower. For that damage. Perfect. What's the total for that, guys?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Math. Wizard math.

preceding_turns.text: Math has no power here.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Nice! As you grab the tentacle, you can see the slippery, slimy flesh, and it has the residue on it. As your fingers grasp into it, its brownish-yellow color all of a sudden turns into this grayish sickly dried landscape, as you can see the flesh cracking to dust around it. The tentacle's still grasping onto you. The magical energy travels up the side of what would be its torso and body, and you can see it beginning to withdraw through severe pain. It manages to eventually shrug off any further spread of the magical disease, of the Blight, but it has left this horrible scarring across one side of its whole body. You can't really move this turn, since you're currently restrained.

preceding_turns.text: Not yet.

preceding_turns.text: 36 damage.

preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.text: Okay. That'll get you there. That one rat swarm does get a free attack on you as you move past it. That is going to be an 11. Pulls you right up in front of it, now. Its giant mouth is (chomping sounds) angrily gnashing and biting down in front of you, and it's sending bits of that brackish spittle across your face and smells horrifying. What are you doing?

preceding_turns.text: I'd like to try to take control of it with Geas. See if I can tell this bitch what to do.

preceding_turns.text: Do it!

preceding_turns.text: Not you.

preceding_turns.text: That is eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, cool. All right.

preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No! (squeaking noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Very unhappy about that. Okay, three. How's that hole looking? It's a great hole, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Just getting warmed up! And a one.

preceding_turns.text: It can't be real! It can't be real.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This time, in an angry fury that you are being stifled by what is essentially sewer rats, you lift your axe up in the air as one rat has climbed up your pants bites you right in the crotch.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Don't touch it, don't do that. Okay. Yes, it does.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, that's not bad. Looking at the stonework here, it's definitely created for function more than any sort of presentory functionality. You gather from looking at the nearby pieces of the stone wall that connects with the first set of metal bars that this was probably carved a few hundred years ago, and the metal bars as you move past-- you guys see this too-- the doorways that lead into these, some appear to be locked and some are completely open, easy to swing, and as you pull it open, (creak) very heavy rust against those iron doors, probably affected largely by the nearby moisture emanating from the room and whatever chemical's hanging in the air.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I'll continue on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you enter this area and start stepping in, the air itself has a very chemical smell to it. It's hard to describe. There's something musty and untouched to the air, but there's also some sort of gentle hint of an acrid scent. Do you guys continue forward through this prison area?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm looking at the floor and everything as far out as I can see ahead of me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's above us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Looking up, it's about 15 feet up, and it is another smooth stone ceiling. The construction here looks old. You, specifically, go ahead and make a perception check. With advantage, because you are a dwarf.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: 12 plus whatever. Do I roll again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, roll again. As you're a dwarf, and this is an underground stone structure.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Oh, nine. So 15.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The lock on this, it's not an intricate lock, but it is very heavily rusted, and as soon as you start moving in there, you realize that there is actually something metal that has been broken off inside, whether by a previous key, just through accident, or intent, it was broken off inside. You manage to find a way to remove it, pull the piece out, and still get the lock open. The gate swings open, and you guys enter to inspect this corpse. Looking at it, you notice that the body has been probably dead for a couple months. It is in prolonged state of decomposition, but there is still some semblance of flesh sloughing off the remnants of the bone. You can see from the wounds that it has received that a large portion of it has been torn open very violently and elements of it have been removed. The softer organs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is balance to the universe. As you're walking past, Keyleth, looking over on one side, you see further up, with a bunch of the thatch pushed around it, there appears to be a corpse that doesn't have the layer of dust that a number of the other skeletal piles in some of the other cells seem to have. The door to get into the cell, though, does appear to be locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vax. Need your help; this is locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What's in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Looks like a fresh dead guy?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Out comes the pick. I'm going to perception check that corpse through the cell. That's a 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's hard to tell. It's mostly covered with a lot of this thatch. It doesn't look like it was intentionally covered, but some sort of a struggle took place in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Now for the door. Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: You're welcome. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I work in mysterious ways.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "That was two weeks ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Are all the guards that come Bastions of the Raven Queen?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We are the ones to protect the Amaranthine Oubliette. That is our job here. You sit in the lower forgotten halls of the Oubliette. My final resting place."

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Stormcloud? Any questions?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I think we've learned all we can from this one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Which direction was the dwarf walking?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You can answer her. What direction was the dwarf going?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The other hand creeps up from underneath the thatch. As you guys are watching this, the corpse is doing nothing, it's just lying there, he is talking to an unmoving corpse. For your vision, the hand reaches over and points in the direction you guys came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: So it went the way we came.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'd like to have everybody roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You (sniffs) and it clears your sinuses up pretty well. Much like a fresh dab of wasabi, your nostrils are a little more open. Because it's not sulfurous, it doesn't have something similar to what you'd find in smithing in your experience, at the very least. It's hard to really place where it comes from. You said you're going which way?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Towards that other door that they did not come out of.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This here? All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 23 I rolled, perception.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23. You approach the door. This door is locked. It does not appear to be trapped.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Let's unlock it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you reach down to the lock and you place the picks inside, start fiddling, you look at the iron around it, and the actual metal seems to be frosting over. You pull back your head, and as you exhale, your breath becomes visible and the temperature seems to drop significantly, and as soon as you look up at the wood, something shimmers in the wood, and it's a humanoid face that just pushes out of the wood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I use Uncanny Dodge to roll backward right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are hearing his reaction to this just happen in the front, there. I'm going to go ahead and say sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So one of the hands comes through, reaching out for you, this ghostly arm just swings towards you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I say, nope!

payload.raw_text: 12. All of you guys manage to shrug off the effect. You, however. There's something about the way it screamed that for a moment, you swear it had a face similar to Vesh from your wedding night. It clutches your heart, and you are considered frightened for the next minute. That means you cannot move closer to it, and your attacks against it are at a disadvantage until you shrug it off. All right, then the possessed Vax is going to, as his bonus action, attempt to make a stealth check and back out of the way. Goes against the wall. If I could have Thorbir and Tiberius make perception checks.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: 23? Okay. You release all three Scorching Rays blasting into the ghost. It's impacting, but the bursts seem to detonate through it. You can see it's taking the hits, but it seems almost like because of its ethereal form, it's not feeling the full brunt of the attacks. It's affected by it, and you can see its form being shuddered around by it, but it's not as effective as you're used to them being. That ends your turn, Tiberius. This creature here. Okay, it does not regain its possession ability. It is going to use Horrifying Visage. All of a sudden, the ghost (roars) Ghostbusters style. It grows and it's just a horrible screaming entity. I need all of you except for Vax to go ahead and make a wisdom saving throw.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: How it works is whenever you cast a new concentration spell, the other one lasts for two rounds, and then vanishes. I think this should be the last round that you have it, because it's been two rounds since you moved back. So this would be the last round that you have it. So you still have it, but it fades after this round. So halve that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wait, he has to roll at disadvantage. Cloak.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right, but he has advantage on you because you didn't see him, so it cancels it out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's true, yeah. My cloak gives you disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so go ahead and roll with sneak attack damage against Tiberius.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That is a nine plus five, ten, 22, 24. That's 33.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 33 points of piercing damage against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What's half of that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No Stoneskin. You've already cast a concentration spell. Right, but when you cast one, the other one lasts for two more rounds, and you started with True Strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's also been way over a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It's an hour-long spell. I can still hold another one and do a different one, and hold the other spell.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You slam your foot into the ground for a second and focus, and for a second the divine energy pulses through your body, and you let a little bit of Vesh's essence seep into you, against your better judgment. It gives you that sense of bravery to shrug off the fear you felt, and for a second there, you're like, as a healing cleric, undead should be afraid of you. And you remember that there's an ability that you have called Turn Undead that may come in handy, going forward. That ends your turn. Keyleth, make another saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: No one hates themselves as much as I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so do you want to move?

preceding_turns.text: I can only move back and forth, right? Then let me stay there so I can concentrate as much as I can on holding the blessing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both fail. All right. So suddenly, as the blast wave hits Vax, the spirit is forced out of his body against the wall. It pushes it out, and all of a sudden, you have control of your form again. Blown against the wall, and they both halfway slide into the wall. They both have a severe look of fear on their face. That's going to end your turn. Keyleth, make another saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So a second failed death saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Someone pump some life into me!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Tiberius.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to pop one of my simple potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, just the level one?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, go ahead and roll. That is 2d4 plus two.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That's seven.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Superior's another 4d4, yeah. You guys can take a short rest, if you wish to, and also use your hit dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait. Vax, slow down.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I follow him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I do not follow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do not follow? Okay, Tiberius stays back. You guys are all staying there?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to call everyone together, if I could, first. Let's take a moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just looking. I take another potion, because I have one more. And I'm going to look down the hall.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Wait, friend. I give him a potion of superior healing.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You know I'm a healer, right? You can save your potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Save it. What's that? Superior is a 4d4?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Marisha. Just a heads-up. One of the material components is a gem-encrusted bowl that costs 1000 gold pieces that is consumed by it. You can totally cast this, but you'll have to deduct a thousand gold off your personal gold supply.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I put my hand on Thorbir's hand and guide him back out and shut the door and say, it's fucking acid in that room. I just sit on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Of course it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vax?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm back in the hall with you. Acid-filled room. I'm going to rest. Let's all just take a breather.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can we make camp? I'm hurting really bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Let's take a moment here.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I agree. We've been hit pretty hard, pretty quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to camp, or do you want to take a breather?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just take a rest. Actually, I have something that I think will make us all feel better. And I prepare a Heroes' Feast.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You have those?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I do now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What does that do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll tell you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Are there bananas?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a 20-foot-long hoagie.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, it's the 20-foot-long Subway dealio.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: No, not Subway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Too soon. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'd say, with shopping time, it's probably been about six hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Then you don't have to worry about it. You're fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why did you burn all of your sorcerer points? We told you not to burn all of your sorcerer points. We don't have time to sit here and take a full rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I can still cure everybody, you know. I can heal everybody. The wounds.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Use your hit dice to heal yourself. You commence on the meal, and it is a very delightful meal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You can ask the group, but I have a feeling that Vax and Thorbir aren't going to want to take a full rest down here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's going to get gone. That guy or that gal.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Both of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: It's a thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't want to go on a date with it. I want to kill it.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: So while we're sitting here, can I cast Locate Creature? Oh, wait. Here's a question that I have. How much time has passed since the last time I cast it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You finish Locate Creature, and you sit there, and you can sense, grasping on that one encounter you had with the rakshasa, though it was still disguised as a dwarf, and the descriptor you have. And you recognize this immediately. This is the same type of ritual that you did earlier, and so there's now this weird, synchronous moment that you share magical knowledge.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: It is legend.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: The acid guy is legend? All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I go walking down the hallway, this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Keyleth, what are you doing? You're meditating?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let me wait until he opens this door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I'll make a perception check at that door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. The door does not appear to be trapped. You don't know if it's locked yet. It's hard to tell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just, allow me. You're strong.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: That's what they say.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's 27.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is also, from your perspective, not locked or trapped.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, now I'm going to drop to my knees and cast Locate Creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you're finishing your small ritual for Locate Creature, you open up the door, and it is a spiral staircase that leads upward. You get the sensation this is where those earlier Bastions were coming from.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: No acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No acid in this room, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just a staircase up?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I would say Mass Cure Wounds, as a tactical standpoint, is a better thing to save for combat because it's a thing you can do one round, and you can heal everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why are you being so poopy today, Tiberius?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm cranky because I haven't slept, that's why.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can we just take a moment for the carpet, please? We've had a lot of good times with that carpet.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Too bad, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We've had a lot of good times.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You've had that carpet for a while?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We found Clarota on that carpet. Never mind. Let's not talk about that. (sighs)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Thorbir has no attachment to this carpet. I'm looking for exits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Nor does Tiberius.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, I do. And my sister's going to kill me, specifically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Vex is going to be so angry.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: This is a death march.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I give Vax a hug.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Are we curing the wounds, too?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. What's the total amount of cure? Mass Cure Wounds?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I want to cast on everybody, if I can.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. First off, everyone roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I want him dead. I want his house burned to the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you're all taking in this still and very creepy omnipresence of this Hotis character, you, in hearing the voice, it is a very trained voice. This is an individual that has spent the entirety of their life manipulating and toying with the emotions, minds, greed, and goals of others to get its desires itself, both self-preservation and progressive expansion of its own territory. You can tell in its voice, there is a hint of fear and a very heavy coating of words that are very well practiced and silver-tongued. But you gather that there is very little intent to follow through on its side of the bargain, and everything is designed as misdirection to send whatever it's made a deal with to its eventual doom.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: It dies in this room. Do you hear me? You die in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That is unfortunate." I would like all of you to make a perception check right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: 13 total.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not bad. 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Both Keyleth and Vax, looking around the room, there is a little hint of movement, and you look over at the statue itself. There's a little glimmer of something shift. You see what appears to be this amulet or necklace of some kind that was resting around the statue. It vanishes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, son of a bitch. I want to cast Faerie Fire in that direction, right where I see the glimmer.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There's a big statue between the two of you, unfortunately, the ten-foot statue, so you wouldn't have visual on it when it cast the spell. This is the big statue, right there. So you are currently dominated. It is now your turn. The rakshasa is going to send you up behind Kash.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Advantage! I'm so glad I made such a hearty meal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ugh, 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15 total?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. For the second time in a row, your mind is not your own.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (robotic) I will kill you all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You feel the will of the rakshasa take your mind as you are dominated by it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is this something he can save from, though?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What happened? Is a spell being cast?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm possessed, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Counterspell. 4th level.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You currently don't have visual on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Besides, you should have done that shit already! The moment already happened. I'm possessed.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Shut up. I'm trying to save you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You can't retcon that shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Same team!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 23. You just catch, out of the corner of your eye, as Vax turns towards you, weapons drawn, and swooshes up to your side. You get the sensation that his mind is not his own, since this has already happened once today. Doing so, you turn to face him, negating the sneak attack. You're aware of it. So you don't get sneak attack damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's cut the guest visitor because really. Really?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) It makes sense. He comes in and heals the party and is closest to you and has no idea that you're currently turned against the rest of your allies. Make an attack on him.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I'm attacking him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's attacking you.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: You suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I'm hasted. My boots are hasted. So here I go. Oh, that's good. That is a 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Should I do damage on all of these?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and do your sneak attack damage on this one.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, but there's no sneak attack, so not that bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I will say this. Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I perceive I'm going to get my ass kicked. Oh, good! 23.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You strike. Once again, as it runs by, you get a chunk out of it and it (grunts) growls in anger and pain. You notice at the same time that she takes the bite out of it, the guardian shudders, like it suddenly took an impact on its own. As it charges away, though, it runs out of the field of Silence... It is going to begin casting a spell on itself, and immediately drifts up toward the ceiling. It is now currently flying in the room about ten feet up right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Can I use second wind? It says I can take it as a bonus action, but I don't know how bonus actions work.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get one bonus action every turn, if you have an ability that uses one.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Oh, great. So I get hit points equal to d10 plus my fighter level.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: 11 plus ten is 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go. Very nice. Ends your turn. Back at the top. That is Hotis's turn. Hotis begins looking at itself. It moves. It gets an attack from you, Marisha.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So that does hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Awesome. I'm going to do my bite attack again. Oh, wait. That's a d20. Eight damage. Piercing damage again.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So you roll a d20. Add a five. I will say you have advantage on this because you were expecting and waiting for this, so go ahead and make your first roll. Roll one at a time.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: That's not how it worked before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. That's how my ring works, as well. It's a level-5 Ring of Storing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome to Dungeons and Dragons. It's all good. You have Counterspell as a spell you know, though, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Still 4th-level Counterspell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Good to know. That makes a difference. Let me double-check one thing real fast, because this will make or break the circumstance, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: As a matter of fact, no. 6th-level Counterspell. (growling) You will go nowhere!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: That's far as I can go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We go to 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. It is a 7th-level spell, so let's go ahead and roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Do I have advantage or anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't think so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Don't roll yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) That's awesome. Okay. He's in charge!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so roll a d20 and your spell-casting ability is five for this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't piss off Tetsuo, man.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Gives you advantage on those, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: We need the eyes. I didn't have the contract.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: You had the contract.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I did not. You were in charge.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: I was not in charge. I was never in charge. I was sent here to be a babysitter.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: That's in charge, as far as I'm concerned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Does nobody have the contract?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Is it behind us?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We signed it, after, and left it somewhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It might have ended up in the storage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, the actual, physical--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The actual, physical contract.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Might have ended up in storage.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Someone from production made us sign it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Here it is, clearly, right here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The fangs and the tongue.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: Ah, yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We found it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would have been a real shame to get all this far.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I got it. Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: We got it. It's all good. What do we need?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're fine here. Everything's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: How are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I have my book, which I think gives me advantage, I think is how it works?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (excited chuckles) He shows it to the Huntmaster. She glances down with a smile. "Very good. I am glad that we gave you such a trusted member as your overseer. You did well, Thorbir. Thank you for your effort in keeping these fledglings alive."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: On the way out, I grab two glasses of ale, and I hand one to this guy and just walk and don't say shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No one argues.

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: Thorbir is genuinely touched by this gesture.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you sip, you make your way through the main thoroughfare back to the center of the Quad Roads to the Slayer's Take guildhall. You step inside, and there across the way, in mid-conversation with his wife, is Mertin, talking to the Huntmaster, Vanessa. As you enter, they both turn with a look of excited surprise. "I assume that this means the contract went well, right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: It's done.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I take a canvas bag and I plop it on the table, full of the rakshasa bits.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She crosses her arms and her usual jovial, passive attitude grows a little more stern. She gives a nod of genuine respect towards Thorbir. And for the first time she looks about the rest of you with a slightly intense look of really understanding the essence of strength between and behind each of you. "I believe you're correct. I think there will be great things ahead for us. Welcome to the Slayer's Take." She reaches down, brings up a small wooden chest. Sets it on the edge and opens it. On the inside, you can see it is rather abundant in gold and platinum, of which the total comes to-- (counts quietly) 5,100 gold pieces for this contract. That is 1,020 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Please do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Where are you staying?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where are we staying?

preceding_turns.speaker: WILL
preceding_turns.text: I thought as members of the Take, you got to stay here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You could, if you like. We have room, and technically, as of this contract, you are all officially members of the Take. You may stay here, if you wish."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I need a bed. And I motion towards the thing. I'm sleepy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We'll wait here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. We shall hold off the ceremony for when the rest of your team arrives."

preceding_turns.speaker: WIL
preceding_turns.text: If the rest of this team is half as good as these members, the Take has grown much stronger this day. They're worthy.
